text: Meeting deadlines requires several key skills. Good time management helps you work efficiently, so start by breaking large tasks into smaller, manageable steps. Prioritization is essential—tackle the most urgent or important tasks first, and leave less critical work for later. Scope management means saying “no” to extra tasks that fall outside your original plan, as this protects your focus and energy. Finally, communication is crucial. If you foresee a delay, tell your team or client early; they may adjust expectations or offer support. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines more consistently and reduce stress.
